title: $UHTTP_DEF_PROXY_AUTH
text: $UHTTP_DEF_PROXY_AUTH Assignment setting that determines the default authentication schema to be used by the proxy server (if used) when processing UHTTP SEND requests. $UHTTP_DEF_PROXY_AUTH { = } B | L | N Values B for Basic L for NTLM N for Negotiate, (chooses between NTLM and Kerberos) Defaults Assignment file: Application assignment file Section: [PROXY_SETTINGS] Default value: B if user names are provided in the UHTTP SEND operation The UHTTP SEND operation does not specify a user name and password for a proxy server, the credentials of the current Windows user will be used for authentication. This functionality is not available under Unix. If this setting is omitted, and the SEND operation specifies a user name but no authentication scheme, Basic authentication is used. The default authentication scheme can be overridden by specifying a scheme in the Username parameter of the UHTTP SEND operation. Related Links SEND Send an HTTP request. Authentication with UHTTP The UHTTP component is used to send requests to a web server, often via a proxy server. One or both servers may require user credentials to authenticate the request.
